palette_hint: Off-white background #F5F2EC, dark charcoal text #2B2B2B, forest green accent #3A6B45, and warm amber accent #C47D2E for call-to-action elements.
type_style: Headings in Georgia serif at bold weight, body in system-ui sans-serif at regular weight, giving an authoritative yet approachable conservation feel.
description: Hollow Creek Land Trust is a regional nonprofit protecting 14,000 acres of Appalachian forest, wetland, and farmland through voluntary conservation easements and community stewardship programs.
constraints: Max content width of 960px centered with auto margins and 24px horizontal padding on all pages.
Nav bar uses flexbox with the trust logo/name left-aligned and five nav links right-aligned; background #3A6B45, link text #F5F2EC, active page link underlined.
Footer is a 3-column flex row with columns: mission blurb, quick links, and mailing address; background #2B2B2B, text #F5F2EC, min-height 160px.
Call-to-action buttons use background #C47D2E, text #F5F2EC, border-radius 4px, padding 10px 22px, and no hover effects.
Section headings use Georgia bold at 1.8rem, subheadings at 1.2rem, and body text at 1rem/1.6 line-height throughout all pages.

page_specs.home: Hero section with a full-width banner area (background color #3A6B45, text #F5F2EC) containing the tagline 'Protecting Appalachian Land, One Acre at a Time' and a 'Donate Now' CTA button. Below the hero, a 3-column CSS grid showcases impact statistics: 14,000 acres protected, 38 active easements, and 220 volunteer stewards, each in a bordered card with a short label. A final section includes a 2-column flex layout with a short mission paragraph on the left and a 'Learn About Our Land' CTA button on the right.
page_specs.our-land: Introductory paragraph explaining the three landscape types the trust protects: ridge-top hardwood forest, riparian wetlands along Hollow Creek, and working farmland in the Crestview Valley. A CSS grid of 3 cards, one per landscape type, each with a colored top border (forest green for forest, blue-gray #5B8FA8 for wetlands, amber for farmland), a heading, and 2-sentence description. Ends with a text-only table listing the five largest protected properties by name, acreage, year acquired, and primary ecosystem.
page_specs.get-involved: Page opens with a bold heading 'Join the Work of Conservation' followed by three flex-row sections: Volunteer (describing trail monitoring and invasive species removal days), Donate (explaining annual fund and planned giving), and Easement Landowners (explaining how to inquire about donating a conservation easement). Each section has a heading, 3-4 sentences of specific detail, and a '#C47D2E' CTA button with relevant label. A narrow full-width banner in #3A6B45 with white text promotes the next volunteer workday: 'Spring Creek Cleanup — Saturday, April 19 — Crestview Trailhead 8 AM'.
page_specs.stewardship: Explains the trust's annual monitoring program: every easement property is visited once per year by a trained monitor who documents land conditions against the original baseline report. A 2-column grid lists the eight stewardship principles the trust follows, each as a short titled paragraph. Below that, a simple unordered list details the equipment and protocols used during monitoring visits, including GPS boundary walking, photo documentation at 12 fixed points, and written landowner communication within 30 days. Page closes with a paragraph on the trust's partnership with Appalachian State University's ecology department for long-term soil and canopy research.
page_specs.contact: Page header states 'Reach the Hollow Creek Land Trust Team' with the physical address (214 Ridgeline Road, Boone, NC 28607), phone number (828-555-0174), and general email (info@hollowcreeklandtrust.org) displayed in a centered flex column. A 2-column flex layout below places a simple HTML contact form (name, email, subject dropdown with options: Volunteer, Easement Inquiry, Donation, Press, Other, and message textarea) on the left and staff directory on the right listing four staff members with title and direct email. Form submit button uses the standard #C47D2E style; no JS required.
